title: phegopteris dryopteris subsp. robertiana
intro: Phegopteris dryopteris subsp. robertiana, commonly known as Robert's Lady Fern, is a subspecies of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Phegopteris dryopteris subsp. robertiana include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and act as an expectorant to relieve coughs and congestion. In herbal preparations, the leaves of this plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
